figures.caption: Figure 1: CPDAGs returned by BOSS+FFML on the Auto MPG dataset (n = 392, one discrete variable: origin). Directed edges (→) indicate orientations determined from the data or background knowledge; undirected edges (—) indicate Markov-equivalent pairs that cannot be oriented from the data alone. (a) No background knowledge; modelyear is treated as endogenous and oriented as a consequence of mpg and weight, which is causally implausible. (b) modelyear constrained to be exogenous via a prior knowledge tier; the resulting graph is both causally more plausible and achieves a higher FFML score (−618.26 vs. −631.88), indicating that the constrained search found a better region of the score landscape.
